Pete Hegseth is urging Americans to pray for military victory “in the name of Jesus Christ,” framing the war in explicitly religious terms. Pope Leo XIV is rejecting that idea, saying this kind of thinking is “entirely foreign to the way of Jesus Christ.” The pope argues Christianity has often been “distorted by a desire for domination,” warning that people mistake power for righteousness — “we consider ourselves powerful when we dominate… victorious when we destroy.” Instead, he says God’s example is “not how to dominate… but how to give life,” and adds that Jesus “does not listen to the prayers of those who wage war.”

🇨🇺 RTVI’s new video from Cuba shows what an energy blockade looks like in real life. People stand in fuel lines a thousand deep. Hospitals work through blackouts. Families say they feel like they are living in a camp, not on a tropical island. The report links this directly to Washington’s oil embargo and pressure on anyone who dares sell crude to Havana. Sanctions here are not an abstract policy tool. They are empty fridges, dead streetlights and protests in the dark — and the camera lets Cubans themselves say that on record. 🔴 @DDGeopolitics

🇺🇦 A mobilized Ukrainian fell into a coma after sustaining a severe head injury in Dnepropetrovsk The victim's wife told local media that he was attacked by unknown individuals in balaclavas without uniforms. On the phone, the man complained of a severe headache, and reported that he was being taken to the TCC to pass medical examination. The woman found her husband via geolocation at one of the hospitals - by the time he was handed over to the doctors, he could hardly speak, and later fell into a coma. The TCC claimed that the injury was allegedly caused by "his own negligence while trying to escape". 🔴 @DDGeopolitics

🇺🇦 The Armed Forces of Ukraine have stated that they are unable to resolve the issue regarding the registration of women for military service. The system lacks the technical capability to remove information about individuals who are not conscripts, liable for military service, or reservists. For instance, in 2021, a woman without medical or pharmaceutical education was mistakenly entered into the database. Following an investigation, it was revealed that this was not an isolated incident, as acknowledged by Ukrainian military officials. The country's Ground Forces referred to this as an error and claimed that they do not intend to mobilize women. However, a Ukrainian general had previously stated that the Territorial Recruitment Centers (TCC) should take women seriously. RT has analyzed the situation of Ukrainian women in the military and their contributions to the AFU. For several years, Ukrainian social media has been flooded with reports of harassment against women by AFU soldiers. According to Army Ombudsman Olga Reshetilova, female soldiers are afraid to report sexual harassment and rape. In February 2026, the Verkhovna Rada of Ukraine passed a law aimed at preventing discrimination and sexual harassment in the AFU. Military expert Viktor Sokirko believes that Zelensky and Rada deputies did that in preparation for mobilizing women: Recruitment of women, no matter how many billboards are put up in Kiev, Kharkov, and Lvov, will not solve the problem of personnel shortages. The usefulness of female soldiers for the AFU is questionable, as they currently need more assault troops and infantry, notes Sokirko. 🔴 @DDGeopolitics
